foreground.h(3) Library Functions Manual foreground.h(3)

NAME

foreground.h

SYNOPSIS

Functions


char * image_print (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_color (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_256color (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_16color (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_16color_dithered (const image_t *p, const char *palette)
char * image_print_16color_dithered_with_background (const image_t *image, bool use_background, const char *palette)

Function Documentation

char * image_print (const image_t * p, const char * palette)

Definition at line 27 of file foreground.c.

27                                                          {
28   if (!p || !palette) {
29     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: p=%p or palette=%p is NULL", p, palette);
30     return NULL;
31   }
32   if (!p->pixels) {
33     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: p->pixels is NULL");
34     return NULL;
35   }
36 
37   const int h = p->h;
38   const int w = p->w;
39 
40   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
41     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image_print: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
42     return NULL;
43   }
44 
45   // Get UTF-8 character cache for proper multi-byte character support
46   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
47   if (!utf8_cache) {
48     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 palette cache for scalar rendering");
49     return NULL;
50   }
51 
52   // Character index ramp is now part of UTF-8 cache - no separate cache needed
53 
54   // Need space for h rows with UTF-8 characters, plus h-1 newlines, plus null terminator
55   const size_t max_char_bytes = 4; // Max UTF-8 character size
56 
57   const rgb_pixel_t *pix = p->pixels;
58 
59   // Use outbuf_t for efficient UTF-8 RLE emission (same as SIMD renderers)
60   outbuf_t ob = {0};
61 
62   // Calculate buffer size with overflow checking
63   size_t w_times_bytes;
64   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)w, max_char_bytes, &w_times_bytes) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
65     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: width too large for UTF-8 encoding");
66     return NULL;
67   }
68 
69   size_t w_times_bytes_plus_one;
70   if (checked_size_add(w_times_bytes, 1, &w_times_bytes_plus_one) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
71     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: width * bytes + 1 overflow");
72     return NULL;
73   }
74 
75   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, w_times_bytes_plus_one, &ob.cap) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
76     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Buffer size overflow: height * (width * bytes + 1) overflow");
77     return NULL;
78   }
79 
80   ob.buf = SAFE_MALLOC(ob.cap ? ob.cap : 1, char *);
81   if (!ob.buf) {
82     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate output buffer for scalar rendering");
83     return NULL;
84   }
85 
86   // Process pixels with UTF-8 RLE emission (same approach as SIMD)
87   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
88     const int row_offset = y * w;
89 
90     for (int x = 0; x < w;) {
91       const rgb_pixel_t pixel = pix[row_offset + x];
92       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
93       const int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
94 
95       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
96       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
97       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
98       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
99 
100       // Use same 64-entry cache as SIMD for consistency
101       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache64[luma_idx];
102 
103       // Find run length for same character (RLE optimization)
104       int j = x + 1;
105       while (j < w) {
106         const rgb_pixel_t next_pixel = pix[row_offset + j];
107         const int next_luminance = (77 * next_pixel.r + 150 * next_pixel.g + 29 * next_pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
108         uint8_t next_safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(next_luminance);
109         uint8_t next_luma_idx = (uint8_t)(next_safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
110         uint8_t next_char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[next_luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
111         if (next_char_idx != char_idx)
112           break;
113         j++;
114       }
115       uint32_t run = (uint32_t)(j - x);
116 
117       // Emit UTF-8 character with RLE (same as SIMD)
118       ob_write(&ob, (const char *)char_info->utf8_bytes, char_info->byte_len);
119       if (rep_is_profitable(run)) {
120         emit_rep(&ob, run - 1);
121       } else {
122         for (uint32_t k = 1; k < run; k++) {
123           ob_write(&ob, (const char *)char_info->utf8_bytes, char_info->byte_len);
124         }
125       }
126       x = j;
127     }
128 
129     // Add newline between rows (except last row)
130     if (y != h - 1) {
131       ob_putc(&ob, '\n');
132     }
133   }
134 
135   ob_term(&ob);
136   return ob.buf;
137 }








char
  * image_print_16color (const image_t * p, const char *
  palette)


Definition at line 472 of file foreground.c.


472                                                                      {
473   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
474     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
475     return NULL;
476   }
477 
478   int h = image->h;
479   int w = image->w;
480 
481   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
482     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
483     return NULL;
484   }
485 
486   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
487   ansi_fast_init_16color();
488 
489   // Calculate buffer size (smaller than 256-color due to shorter ANSI sequences)
490   // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
491   // Calculate with overflow checking
492   size_t h_times_w;
493   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, (size_t)w, &h_times_w) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
494     return NULL;
495   }
496 
497   size_t h_times_w_times_12;
498   if (checked_size_mul(h_times_w, 12u, &h_times_w_times_12) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
499     return NULL;
500   }
501 
502   size_t buffer_size;
503   if (checked_size_add(h_times_w_times_12, (size_t)h, &buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
504     return NULL;
505   }
506 
507   char *buffer;
508   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
509 
510   char *ptr = buffer;
511   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
512 
513   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
514   if (!utf8_cache) {
515     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 16-color rendering");
516     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
517     return NULL;
518   }
519 
520   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
521     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
522       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
523 
524       // Convert RGB to 16-color index and generate ANSI sequence
525       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b);
526       ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
527 
528       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
529       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
530 
531       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
532       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
533       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
534       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
535 
536       // Use direct palette character lookup (same as SIMD would do)
537       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
538       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
539 
540       if (char_info) {
541         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
542         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
543           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
544         }
545       } else {
546         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
547         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
548         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
549         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
550       }
551     }
552 
553     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
554     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
555     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
556     if (y < h - 1) {
557       *ptr++ = '\n';
558     }
559   }
560 
561   *ptr = '\0';
562   return buffer;
563 }










char
  * image_print_16color_dithered (const image_t * p, const char *
  palette)


Definition at line 589 of file foreground.c.


589                                                                               {
590   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
591     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
592     return NULL;
593   }
594 
595   int h = image->h;
596   int w = image->w;
597 
598   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
599     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color_dithered: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
600     return NULL;
601   }
602 
603   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
604   ansi_fast_init_16color();
605 
606   // Allocate error buffer for Floyd-Steinberg dithering
607   size_t pixel_count = (size_t)h * (size_t)w;
608   rgb_error_t *error_buffer;
609   error_buffer = SAFE_CALLOC(pixel_count, sizeof(rgb_error_t), rgb_error_t *);
610 
611   // Calculate buffer size (same as non-dithered version)
612   // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
613   // Calculate with overflow checking
614   size_t h_times_w2;
615   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, (size_t)w, &h_times_w2) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
616     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
617     return NULL;
618   }
619 
620   size_t h_times_w2_times_12;
621   if (checked_size_mul(h_times_w2, 12u, &h_times_w2_times_12) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
622     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
623     return NULL;
624   }
625 
626   size_t buffer_size;
627   if (checked_size_add(h_times_w2_times_12, (size_t)h, &buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
628     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
629     return NULL;
630   }
631 
632   char *buffer;
633   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
634 
635   char *ptr = buffer;
636   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
637 
638   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
639   if (!utf8_cache) {
640     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 16-color dithered rendering");
641     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
642     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
643     return NULL;
644   }
645 
646   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
647     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
648       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
649 
650       // Convert RGB to 16-color index using dithering
651       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color_dithered(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b, x, y, w, h, error_buffer);
652       ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
653 
654       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
655       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
656 
657       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
658       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
659       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);        // 0-63 index (same as SIMD)
660       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx]; // Map to character index (same as SIMD)
661 
662       // Use direct palette character lookup (same as SIMD would do)
663       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
664       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
665 
666       if (char_info) {
667         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
668         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
669           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
670         }
671       } else {
672         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
673         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
674         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
675         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
676       }
677     }
678 
679     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
680     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
681     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
682     if (y < h - 1) {
683       *ptr++ = '\n';
684     }
685   }
686 
687   *ptr = '\0';
688   SAFE_FREE(error_buffer); // Clean up error buffer
689   return buffer;
690 }










char
  * image_print_16color_dithered_with_background (const image_t * image,
  bool use_background, const char * palette)


Definition at line 693 of file foreground.c.


693                                                                                                                    {
694   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
695     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
696     return NULL;
697   }
698 
699   int h = image->h;
700   int w = image->w;
701 
702   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
703     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_16color_dithered_with_background: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
704     return NULL;
705   }
706 
707   // Initialize 16-color lookup table
708   ansi_fast_init_16color();
709 
710   // Allocate error buffer for Floyd-Steinberg dithering
711   size_t pixel_count = (size_t)h * (size_t)w;
712   rgb_error_t *error_buffer;
713   error_buffer = SAFE_CALLOC(pixel_count, sizeof(rgb_error_t), rgb_error_t *);
714 
715   // Calculate buffer size (larger for background mode due to more ANSI sequences)
716   size_t buffer_size =
717       (size_t)h * (size_t)w * (use_background ? 24 : 12) + (size_t)h; // Space for ANSI codes + newlines
718   char *buffer;
719   buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
720 
721   char *ptr = buffer;
722   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
723 
724   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
725   if (!utf8_cache) {
726     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 16-color dithered bg rendering");
727     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
728     SAFE_FREE(error_buffer);
729     return NULL;
730   }
731 
732   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
733     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
734       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
735 
736       // Convert RGB to 16-color index using dithering
737       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_16color_dithered(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b, x, y, w, h, error_buffer);
738 
739       if (use_background) {
740         // Background mode: use contrasting foreground on background color
741         uint8_t bg_r, bg_g, bg_b;
742         get_16color_rgb(color_index, &bg_r, &bg_g, &bg_b);
743 
744         // Calculate luminance to choose contrasting foreground
745         int bg_luminance = (bg_r * 77 + bg_g * 150 + bg_b * 29) / 256;
746         uint8_t fg_color = (bg_luminance < 127) ? 15 : 0; // White on dark, black on bright
747 
748         ptr = append_16color_bg(ptr, color_index); // Set background to pixel color
749         ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, fg_color);    // Set contrasting foreground
750       } else {
751         // Foreground mode: set foreground to pixel color
752         ptr = append_16color_fg(ptr, color_index);
753       }
754 
755       // Use same luminance formula as SIMD: ITU-R BT.601 with rounding
756       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
757 
758       // Use same 6-bit precision as SIMD: map luminance (0-255) to bucket (0-63) then to character
759       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
760       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);
761       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx];
762 
763       // Use direct palette character lookup (same as SIMD would do)
764       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
765       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
766 
767       if (char_info) {
768         // Copy UTF-8 character bytes
769         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
770           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
771         }
772       } else {
773         // Fallback to simple ASCII if UTF-8 cache fails
774         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
775         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
776         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
777       }
778     }
779 
780     // Add reset and newline at end of each row
781     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
782     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
783     if (y < h - 1) {
784       *ptr++ = '\n';
785     }
786   }
787 
788   *ptr = '\0';
789   SAFE_FREE(error_buffer); // Clean up error buffer
790   return buffer;
791 }










char
  * image_print_256color (const image_t * p, const char *
  palette)


Definition at line 367 of file foreground.c.


367                                                                       {
368   if (!image || !image->pixels || !palette) {
369     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "image=%p or image->pixels=%p or palette=%p is NULL", image, image->pixels, palette);
370     return NULL;
371   }
372 
373   int h = image->h;
374   int w = image->w;
375 
376   if (h <= 0 || w <= 0) {
377     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "image_print_256color: invalid dimensions h=%d, w=%d", h, w);
378     return NULL;
379   }
380 
381   ansi_fast_init_256color();
382 
383   // Buffer size: 256-color ANSI codes are "\033[38;5;NNNm" (max 13 bytes) + char + reset + newline
384   size_t h_times_w;
385   if (checked_size_mul((size_t)h, (size_t)w, &h_times_w) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
386     return NULL;
387   }
388 
389   size_t h_times_w_times_18;
390   if (checked_size_mul(h_times_w, 18u, &h_times_w_times_18) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
391     return NULL;
392   }
393 
394   size_t buffer_size;
395   if (checked_size_add(h_times_w_times_18, (size_t)h, &buffer_size) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
396     return NULL;
397   }
398 
399   char *buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(buffer_size, char *);
400   char *ptr = buffer;
401   const char *reset_code = "\033[0m";
402 
403   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
404   if (!utf8_cache) {
405     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 cache for 256-color rendering");
406     SAFE_FREE(buffer);
407     return NULL;
408   }
409 
410   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
411     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
412       rgb_pixel_t pixel = image->pixels[y * w + x];
413 
414       uint8_t color_index = rgb_to_256color(pixel.r, pixel.g, pixel.b);
415       ptr = append_256color_fg(ptr, color_index);
416 
417       int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
418 
419       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
420       uint8_t luma_idx = (uint8_t)(safe_luminance >> 2);
421       uint8_t char_idx = utf8_cache->char_index_ramp[luma_idx];
422 
423       char ascii_char = palette[char_idx];
424       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[(unsigned char)ascii_char];
425 
426       if (char_info) {
427         for (int byte_idx = 0; byte_idx < char_info->byte_len; byte_idx++) {
428           *ptr++ = char_info->utf8_bytes[byte_idx];
429         }
430       } else {
431         size_t palette_len = strlen(palette);
432         int palette_index = (luminance * ((int)palette_len - 1) + 127) / 255;
433         *ptr++ = palette[palette_index];
434       }
435     }
436 
437     SAFE_STRNCPY(ptr, reset_code, 5);
438     ptr += strlen(reset_code);
439     if (y < h - 1) {
440       *ptr++ = '\n';
441     }
442   }
443 
444   *ptr = '\0';
445   return buffer;
446 }










char
  * image_print_color (const image_t * p, const char * palette)


Converts an image to colored ASCII art with ANSI escape codes.




This function generates a string representation of an image where
    each pixel is converted to an ASCII character with ANSI color codes. The
    character is chosen based on luminance, and colors are applied using 24-bit
    RGB ANSI escape sequences.




Buffer allocation is precisely calculated to avoid waste and
    prevent overflows:


    
  
  • Each pixel: 1 ASCII char + foreground ANSI code (19 bytes max)
    • 
  
  • Background mode: adds background ANSI code (19 bytes max per pixel)
    • 
  
  • Each row: reset sequence (\033[0m = 4 bytes) + newline (except last
    row)
    • 
  
  • At the end: null terminator (1 byte)
    • 



Color modes:


    
  
  • Foreground only (default): ASCII characters with colored foreground
    • 
  
  • Background mode (opt_background_color): colored background with
      contrasting foreground (black on bright backgrounds, white on dark
      backgrounds)
    • 



ANSI escape code format:


    
  
  • Foreground: \033[38;2;R;G;Bm (11-19 bytes depending on RGB values)
    • 
  
  • Background: \033[48;2;R;G;Bm (11-19 bytes depending on RGB values)
    • 
  
  • Reset: \033[0m (4 bytes)
    • 



Parameters


p Pointer to image_t structure containing
  pixel data


Returns


Dynamically allocated string containing colored ASCII
  art, or NULL on error. Caller is responsible for freeing the returned
  string.


Note


The function performs overflow checks to prevent integer
  overflow when calculating buffer sizes for very large images.



Uses global opt_background_color to determine color mode.




Exits with ERROR_BUFFER if buffer overflow is detected during
    string construction (should never happen with correct calculation).




Definition at line 191 of file foreground.c.


191                                                                {
192   if (!p || !palette) {
193     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "p=%p or palette=%p is NULL", p, palette);
194     return NULL;
195   }
196   if (!p->pixels) {
197     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "p->pixels is NULL");
198     return NULL;
199   }
200 
201   // Get UTF-8 character cache for proper multi-byte character support
202   utf8_palette_cache_t *utf8_cache = get_utf8_palette_cache(palette);
203   if (!utf8_cache) {
204     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Failed to get UTF-8 palette cache for scalar color rendering");
205     return NULL;
206   }
207 
208   const int h = p->h;
209   const int w = p->w;
210 
211   // Constants for ANSI escape codes (using exact sizes from ansi_fast.c)
212   const size_t max_fg_ansi = 19; // \033[38;2;255;255;255m
213   const size_t max_bg_ansi = 19; // \033[48;2;255;255;255m
214   const size_t reset_len = 4;    // \033[0m
215 
216   const size_t h_sz = (size_t)h;
217   const size_t w_sz = (size_t)w;
218 
219   // Ensure h * w won't overflow
220   if (h_sz > 0 && w_sz > SIZE_MAX / h_sz) {
221     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Image dimensions too large: %d x %d", h, w);
222     return NULL;
223   }
224 
225   const size_t total_pixels = h_sz * w_sz;
226   const size_t bytes_per_pixel = 1 + max_fg_ansi + max_bg_ansi; // Conservative estimate for max possible
227 
228   // Ensure total_pixels * bytes_per_pixel won't overflow
229   if (total_pixels > SIZE_MAX / bytes_per_pixel) {
230     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Pixel data too large for buffer: %d x %d", h, w);
231     return NULL;
232   }
233 
234   const size_t pixel_bytes = total_pixels * bytes_per_pixel;
235 
236   // Per row: newline (except last row)
237   // Final reset added once by ansi_rle_finish()
238   const size_t total_newlines = (h_sz > 0) ? (h_sz - 1) : 0;
239   const size_t final_reset = reset_len; // One reset at end (from ansi_rle_finish)
240 
241   // Final buffer size: pixel bytes + per-row newlines + final reset + null terminator
242   const size_t extra_bytes = total_newlines + final_reset + 1;
243 
244   if (pixel_bytes > SIZE_MAX - extra_bytes) {
245     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Final buffer size would overflow: %d x %d", h, w);
246     return NULL;
247   }
248 
249   const size_t lines_size = pixel_bytes + extra_bytes;
250   char *lines;
251   lines = SAFE_MALLOC(lines_size, char *);
252 
253   const rgb_pixel_t *pix = p->pixels;
254   // char *current_pos = lines;
255   // const char *buffer_end = lines + lines_size - 1; // reserve space for '\0'
256 
257   // Initialize the optimized RLE context for color sequence caching
258   // Note: This function should be called via image_print_with_capabilities() for proper per-client rendering
259   ansi_rle_context_t rle_ctx;
260   ansi_color_mode_t color_mode = ANSI_MODE_FOREGROUND; // Default to foreground-only for legacy usage
261   ansi_rle_init(&rle_ctx, lines, lines_size, color_mode);
262 
263   // Process each pixel using the optimized RLE context
264   for (int y = 0; y < h; y++) {
265     const int row_offset = y * w;
266 
267     for (int x = 0; x < w; x++) {
268       const rgb_pixel_t pixel = pix[row_offset + x];
269 #ifdef __EMSCRIPTEN__
270       // In WASM/xterm.js builds, quantize RGB to reduce unique colors.
271       // xterm.js batches consecutive same-colored cells into single draw calls,
272       // so fewer unique colors = fewer batches = dramatically faster rendering.
273       // 4 levels per channel (64 colors) keeps good visual quality since
274       // luminance-based character selection uses full-precision values.
275       int r = pixel.r & 0xC0, g = pixel.g & 0xC0, b = pixel.b & 0xC0;
276 #else
277       int r = pixel.r, g = pixel.g, b = pixel.b;
278 #endif
279       // Standard ITU-R BT.601 luminance calculation (uses original values for accuracy)
280       const int luminance = (77 * pixel.r + 150 * pixel.g + 29 * pixel.b + 128) >> 8;
281 
282       // Use UTF-8 character cache for proper character selection
283       uint8_t safe_luminance = clamp_rgb(luminance);
284       const utf8_char_t *char_info = &utf8_cache->cache[safe_luminance];
285 
286       // For RLE, we need to pass the first byte of the UTF-8 character
287       // Note: RLE system may need updates for full UTF-8 support
288       const char ascii_char = char_info->utf8_bytes[0];
289 
290       ansi_rle_add_pixel(&rle_ctx, (uint8_t)r, (uint8_t)g, (uint8_t)b, ascii_char);
291     }
292 
293     // Add newline after each row (except the last row)
294     if (y != h - 1 && rle_ctx.length < rle_ctx.capacity - 1) {
295       rle_ctx.buffer[rle_ctx.length++] = '\n';
296     }
297   }
298 
299   ansi_rle_finish(&rle_ctx);
300 
301   return lines;
302 }












Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.4
    ascii-chat
